rulings.Case Title: JASON TRAVIS VS. HARRIETT M WILSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug 30, 2019 line 2. DEFENDANT HARRIETT WILSON, THE HARRIETT M. WILSON TRUST MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication DENIED. Triable issues of fact regarding Defendant's obligation to provide habitable premises/quiet enjoyment and Defendant's knowledge of the conditions of the property immediately prior to and during Plaintiff's tenancy. These facts are material to all causes of action asserted by Plaintiff. Plaintiff to prepare an order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) and CRC 3.1312. Motion for Summary Adjuration is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is GRANTED as to the cause of action for negligent infliction of emotional distress; duplicative of negligence cause of action. Plaintiff's argument that Stoiber "endorsed the plaintiff's claim for intentional infliction of emotional distress" is inapposite. Stoiber v. Honeychuck (1980) 101 Cal.App.3d 903. Motion for Summary Adjudication is otherwise denied for the reasons stated above. = (501/CFH)
